landmarks.name: 1. Maxi-Lecture Hall
landmarks.desc: The central lecture hall for the Huelva Campus, serving as a hub for academic gatherings.

landmarks.name: 2. Monastery of La Rábida
landmarks.desc: A historic Franciscan monastery deeply connected to the voyages of Christopher Columbus.

landmarks.name: 3. Amphitheater
landmarks.desc: An open-air venue designed for local events and cultural presentations.

landmarks.name: 4. UNIA
landmarks.desc: The Universidad Internacional de Andalucía campus, characterized by its distinct modern and regional architecture.

landmarks.name: 5. Carabelas
landmarks.desc: The docking site of the replica ships from the 1492 expedition.

landmarks.name: 6. Monument of Plus Ultra
landmarks.desc: A tribute to the transatlantic flight of the Plus Ultra seaplane.

landmarks.name: 7. Monumento a los Descubridores
landmarks.desc: A towering monument commemorating the explorers of the Americas.

stops.name: Start of the Path
stops.desc: Looking northeast from here, you can see the Maxi-Lecture Hall of the Huelva Campus in the distance.

stops.name: Estero de Domingo Rubio
stops.desc: Deep in the marshland. Looking east provides a wide view of the UNIA campus grounds across the water.

stops.name: Amphitheater Viewpoint
stops.desc: Looking straight across the terrain to the east reveals both the Amphitheater and the historic Monastery of La Rábida.

stops.name: Southern Edge
stops.desc: The path turns back inland, approaching the historic southern sector where you can clearly see the Monument of Plus Ultra.
